name: Transformers Model Integration
trigger: transformers, Qwen, LLaMA, Gemma, model, language model, LLM inference
approach: Inject custom kernels into transformers models by patching specific layer modules. Walk model.named_modules() to find target layers (e.g., RMSNorm in decoder layers). For models like Qwen3-8B with 65 RMSNorm modules across 32 layers, patching provides cumulative speedup. Register kernels as PyTorch custom ops for torch.compile compatibility. Use model.config for hidden_size, num_layers.
pitfalls: Different model families use different RMSNorm class names (LlamaRMSNorm, Qwen2RMSNorm, etc.)
Epsilon attribute name varies: variance_epsilon (LLaMA/Qwen) vs eps (generic)
Speedup scales with sequence length — 1.58x at 128 tokens but 2.47x at 8192 tokens
RMSNorm is typically ~5% of total compute — a 2x kernel speedup yields ~5% end-to-end
Must handle both float16 and bfloat16 — check model.config.torch_dtype
